name: bluelog
title: bluelog
description:
Bluetooth scanner and logger
Bluelog is a Bluetooth scanner designed to tell you how
many discoverable devices there are in an area as quickly
as possible. It is intended to be used as a site survey
tool, identifying the number of possible Bluetooth targets
there are in the surrounding environment.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use bluelog because Bluetooth scanner and logger.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use bluelog as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `bluelog -i [Interface] [Options] -o [Output File]` as documented upstream for bluelog.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `bluelog -help` as documented upstream for bluelog.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair bluelog with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `bluelog --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install bluelog. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `bluelog -i [Interface] [Options] -o [Output File]`. Further invocations from upstream: `bluelog -help`.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: Bluetooth scanner and logger.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool because: Bluetooth scanner and logger.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install bluelog
Install with: sudo apt install bluelog
